metadata.name: notebooklm-mcp
metadata.description: Unofficial MCP server for Google NotebookLM — bridges AI agents with notebooks programmatically via reverse-engineered internal API

section.name: overview
section.framework: rmcp (MCP server framework), reqwest (HTTP client), clap (CLI)

section.name: architecture

key_modules.module: MCP server setup, CLI commands (21), tool registration (20 tools), request routing
HTTP client for Google NotebookLM API (batchexecute, 20+ methods)
Defensive JSON parser (anti-XSSI, extract_by_rpc_id, zero unwrap)
Artifact type enums (9 types), status codes, payload builders
Payload builders for 5 source types (text, URL, YouTube, Drive, file)
Notebook lifecycle types, parsers (share, summary, details)
CSRF + session token extraction from HTML
Async polling for artifact generation completion
Async polling for source indexing completion

design_patterns.pattern: All Google API calls: batchexecute() → strip_antixssi() → extract_by_rpc_id() → defensive parse
Each #[tool] method makes one RPC call, returns formatted string. Stateless server.
Write operations read back confirmed data after mutation
Token bucket via governor (2s period, ~30 req/min), exponential backoff
Zero unwrap() on external data — all parse functions return Option/Result
OS keyring via keyring crate, DPAPI fallback on Windows

section.data_flow: CLI/MCP client → NotebookLmClient → batchexecute() HTTP POST → Google RPC → strip_antixssi → extract_by_rpc_id → parse → structured result → formatted string response

section.name: api
section.cli_commands: auth-browser, auth-status, verify, list, create, delete, get, rename, summary, share-status, share-set, source-add, source-add-url, source-add-youtube, source-add-drive, source-add-file, artifact-list, artifact-generate, artifact-delete, artifact-download, ask
section.artifact_types: Report (PDF), Quiz (PDF), Flashcards (PDF), Audio (audio file), Infographic (PNG), SlideDeck (PDF/PPTX), MindMap (JSON), Video (video file), DataTable (PDF)

entities.entity: id, title, type, status, task_id, content_url, metadata

section.name: dependencies
section.runtime: tokio, reqwest (rustls), rmcp, clap, governor, serde/serde_json, headless_chrome, keyring, tracing, windows-dpapi
section.external_services: Google NotebookLM internal RPC API (batchexecute endpoint), Google Chrome (via CDP for auth)

confidence_index.high_confidence: Most architecture, API, data models, security from direct source reading
confidence_index.medium_confidence: Temporal period boundaries, some design pattern inferences
confidence_index.low_confidence: Credential storage fallback behavior, source poller edge cases
